description: Create particle effects and combat visual feedback using Canvas drawing calls. Includes muzzle flash, bullet trails, melee slash arcs, explosions, sparks, and environmental particles (steam, floating dust).
acceptance_criteria: Muzzle flash: a bright 2-frame sprite/circle that appears at the weapon position when a ranged attack fires.
Bullet trail: a thin line drawn from attacker to target position that fades over 3 frames.
Melee slash: an arc sprite/canvas path that sweeps in the attack direction.
Explosion: an expanding circle with orange-red gradient that fades over 5 frames, used for grenades and destructible objects.
Sparks: small pixel particles that scatter from damaged electrical equipment (2-3 pixels, random velocity, short lifespan).
Environmental steam: slow-drifting translucent white particles emitted from vent tiles.
Screen-edge damage flash: the canvas border flashes red briefly when the player takes damage.
All effects are non-blocking and play asynchronously over multiple render frames without pausing the turn system.
spec_section: A.2.4 Animation Budget (Combat FX, Atmospheric particles)
notes: Spec: 'Particle effects for atmosphere (steam, sparks, floating debris in zero-g).' Use simple Canvas arc/fillRect calls with alpha fade — no sprite dependencies. A small particle system class manages active particles.
